channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Caitlin Clark gives stamp of approval to Indiana Fever's offseason moves
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Caitlin Clark stayed relatively quiet as the Indiana Fever embarked on a major offseason overhaul. Stephanie White took over as coach. Amber Cox became general manager. The construction of a new practice facility was announced.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chiefs' Patrick Mahomes leaves out teammate while shaking hands during Super Bowl LIX loss
item.description: While Patrick Mahomes was trying to keep spirits high on the Chiefs' sideline during a brutal loss in Super Bowl LIX, he snubbed one of his veteran teammates during handshakes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sabathia to have Yankees logo on Hall of Fame plaque, Wagner the Astros, Suzuki the Mariners
item.description: COOPERSTOWN, N.Y. (AP) — CC Sabathia will have a New York Yankees logo on the cap of his Hall of Fame plaque and Billy Wagner will have the symbol of the Houston Astros. The hall announced the decisions Monday for all five of this year's inductees.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Reds will wear No. 14 patch on jersey sleeve to honor Pete Rose, who died last year
item.description: CINCINNATI (AP) — Pete Rose may be on baseball’s permanently ineligible list, but the late hit king’s No. 14 will be on Cincinnati Reds’ uniforms this year. Cincinnati posted a photo of the patch on X on Monday with the message “14 on our side all season” and a heart emoji.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tiger Woods withdraws from his signature event as he processes death of his mother
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Tiger Woods abruptly withdrew from the Genesis Invitational at Torrey Pines on Monday, saying he was still processing the death of his mother. Kultida Woods died unexpectedly last Tuesday at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Auburn edges Alabama atop AP Top 25 despite loss, Duke and Florida tied at No. 3; UConn drops out
item.description: Auburn's run at No. 1 is still intact despite a loss to No. 3 Florida. UConn's latest loss has dropped the two-time defending national champions out of the poll for the first time in two years.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Track's proposed eligibility, transgender rules would completely ban Semenya and others
item.description: Track and field moved toward adopting rules that would place athletes assigned female at birth but have higher testosterone levels, like Caster Semenya, under the same set of rules as transgender athletes who were born male and transitioned to female.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Anthony Davis is out with groin strain for second game with Dallas Mavericks amid reports of longer absence
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis has been ruled out of what would have been his second game with the Mavericks amid reports that a groin injury sustained in his Dallas debut could sideline him for multiple weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Super Bowl champ Nick Foles teases Tom Brady after Eagles' latest triumph
item.description: Former Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Nick Foles teased Tom Brady after the team's Super Bowl LIX win against the Kansas City Chiefs on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl halftime performer detained after unfurling Sudanese-Palestinian flag
item.description: A performer in Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime show was detained late Sunday after showing a Sudanese-Palestinian flag on the field.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brewers unveil patch they will wear on their uniforms to honor Bob Uecker
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — The Milwaukee Brewers have unveiled a patch they will wear on their uniform this season to honor longtime broadcaster Bob Uecker, who died last month. The patch will appear on the sleeve of the Brewers’ uniforms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles defense smothers Mahomes and dominates Kansas City in franchise's second Super Bowl win
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Cooper DeJean took an interception to the house, Zack Baun made a pick in his old professional home and the Eagles so thoroughly thrashed Patrick Mahomes that by the time Kendrick Lamar had “Game Over” brightened in the Superdome stands to end his halftime set, it was just as clea
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jalen Hurts quiets any doubters with a Super Bowl MVP
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Jalen Hurts has often been judged by his failings more than his successes over the course of his career. Getting benched at halftime of a college football championship game. Being forced to transfer from Alabama to Oklahoma just to be able to regain a starting job.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What happens to the Super Bowl losers' merch? It's being sent to these 5 countries
item.description: The NFL plans for both outcomes so they're ready regardless of who wins. What that inevitably means is a whole lot of merchandise that's pre-printed and pre-made – but fans won't ever be able to buy it.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eagles win Super Bowl LIX, denying Chiefs history in dominant fashion
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles said no to NFL history from the Kansas City Chiefs on Sunday in New Orleans, as they dominated their opponent to win Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jets tell Aaron Rodgers they're 'moving on' from the QB for next season, report says
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is still contemplating whether to continue his playing career. If he does, it appears it won’t be with the New York Jets. The team has told the 41-year-old quarterback it is “moving on from him,” Fox Sports reported Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Clippers dominate fourth quarter in win over Jazz
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - The Los Angeles Clippers broke open a close game in the fourth quarter, outscoring the Jazz 36-24, en route to a 130-110 win over Utah Saturday night. Jordan Clarkson led the Jazz with 24 points, while Keyonte George added 21 off the bench.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Here's how the new NBA All-Star Game format works
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The NBA All-Star Game is coming to Chase Center for the first time on Feb. 16, but the game will not look like the All-Star Games that NBA fans are accustomed to.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: NCAA changes participation policy on transgender athletes
item.description: The NCAA has changed its participation policy for athletes, keeping transgender students from women's sports
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Increased security in New Orleans ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: More than 125,000 visitors — including President Donald Trump — are expected to converge on the Big Easy this week.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia Halas McCaskey, longtime Bears owner and matriarch, dies at 102, team announces
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — Virginia Halas McCaskey, the longtime owner and matriarch of the Chicago Bears, died Thursday at 102, the team announced in a news release. Virginia served as the owner of the Bears since her father and team founder George S. Halas died on Oct. 31, 1983.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz come back to beat Warriors, 131-128
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Jordan Clarkson scored 31 points and Keyonte George added 26 to lead the Utah Jazz to a 131-128 victory over the Golden State Warriors on Wednesday night. Isaiah Collier added 20 points and 11 assists.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Several student-athletes lock in colleges on first NSD of 2025
item.description: Sean Lewis made it clear he wanted to put a fence around San Diego in the recruiting world. He proved it in the high school realm by bringing in 2024 Iron Hog winner Jett Thomas of La Jolla.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Plateau Valley’s Bevan Brothers Commit to Colorado Mesa
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Two brothers from Plateau Valley High School are taking the next step in their athletic careers, and they won’t have to go far to do it. Jackson and Heston Bevan, both standout multi-sport athletes, are staying close to home.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: SDFC completes Sharp Healthcare Performance Center on Sycuan land
item.description: Almost three years in the making, San Diego FC’s Sharp Healthcare Performance Center is now complete.
item.dc:creator: Julian Del Gaudio, Fox 5

item.title: Influencers, retired Bay Area sports stars headline NBA All-Star Celebrity Game in Oakland
item.description: (KRON) -- The NBA released the list of celebrities that will take the floor in Oakland for the NBA All-Star Celebrity Game on Feb. 14. A combination of influencers, retired athletes, film stars and more will put their basketball skills to test.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4
